claim: For all integers n > 2, Euler's totient phi(n) is even.
For all integers n > 2, phi(n) is divisible by 4 if and only if n is not 1, 2, or a power of 2.
For all n > 2 with n odd, phi(n) is divisible by 2 but not necessarily by 4.
For all n > 2 that are powers of 2, phi(n) is divisible by 2.
For all n > 2 where n has at least two distinct odd prime factors, phi(n) is divisible by 4.
predicate: (n <= 2) or (len([p for p in range(3, int(math.sqrt(n))+1, 2) if is_prime(p) and n % p == 0]) < 2) or (phi(n) % 4 == 0)

claim: For all odd prime p, phi(p) = p - 1 is even.
For all n > 2 that are odd and composite, phi(n) is divisible by at least 2.
For all n > 2, the sum phi(n) + n is odd.
For all odd primes p, phi(p^2) is divisible by p-1.
For all n > 2 with exactly two distinct prime factors q, r both greater than 2, phi(n) is divisible by 4.
predicate: (n <= 2) or (len([p for p in range(3, int(math.sqrt(n))+1) if is_prime(p) and n % p == 0]) != 2) or (phi(n) % 4 == 0)

claim: For all n > 2, phi(n) divides 2n.
For all n > 2, gcd(phi(n), n) divides phi(n).
For all n > 2, phi(n) <= n - 1 with equality only when n is prime.
For all composite n > 2, phi(n) <= n/2.
For all n > 2 with exactly two prime factors p < q, phi(n) divides (p-1)(q-1).
predicate: (n <= 2) or (phi(n) % ((min(p for p in range(2, int(math.sqrt(n))+1) if is_prime(p) and n % p == 0)-1)*(max(p for p in range(2, int(math.sqrt(n))+1) if is_prime(p) and n % p == 0)-1)) != 0 if len([p for p in range(2, int(math.sqrt(n))+1) if is_prime(p) and n % p == 0]) == 2 else True)

claim: For all prime powers p^k with k >= 2 and odd prime p, phi(p^k) is even and divisible by p-1.
predicate: (n <= 2) or (not (is_prime(n) or any(is_prime(p) and n == p**int(math.log(n, p)+0.5) for p in range(2, int(math.sqrt(n))+1)))) or (phi(n) % 2 == 0)

claim: For all odd prime powers p^k with k >= 2, phi(p^k) is divisible by 2(p-1).
predicate: (n <= 2) or (phi(n) % (2 * (next((p-1 for p in range(3, int(n**0.5)+1, 2) if is_prime(p) and all(n % (p**j) == 0 for j in range(1, int(math.log(n, p))+2)) and any(n % (p**(j+1)) != 0 for j in range(1, int(math.log(n, p))+1))), n-1))) == 0 if any(is_prime(p) and n % (p**2) == 0 for p in range(3, int(n**0.5)+1, 2)) else True)

claim: For all n > 2, the product of all positive integers coprime to n and less than n equals n^(phi(n)/2) modulo n when phi(n) is even.
For all odd n > 2, phi(n) + phi(2n) = 2*phi(n).
For all odd n > 2, phi(2n) = phi(n).
For all n > 2 where n = 2^k for k >= 2, phi(n) is divisible by 2^(k-1).
For all n > 4 with at least one odd prime divisor, phi(n) is divisible by at least 2.
predicate: (n <= 4) or (all(not is_prime(p) or p == 2 for p in range(2, int(math.sqrt(n))+1) if n % p == 0)) or (phi(n) % 2 == 0)

claim: For all n > 2, phi(n) and n are not coprime when n has an odd prime factor.
predicate: (n <= 2) or (all(not is_prime(p) or p == 2 for p in range(3, int(math.sqrt(n))+1, 2) if n % p == 0)) or (gcd(phi(n), n) > 1)

claim: For all n > 2 with an odd prime divisor p, gcd(phi(n), n) is divisible by p-1.
predicate: (n <= 2) or (all(not is_prime(p) or p == 2 for p in range(3, int(math.sqrt(n))+1, 2) if n % p == 0)) or (all(p % gcd(phi(n), n) == 0 or p == next((q for q in range(3, int(math.sqrt(n))+1, 2) if is_prime(q) and n % q == 0), n) for p in range(2, gcd(phi(n), n)+1)))

claim: For all prime p, phi(2p) = phi(p) if p is odd.
For all n > 2, the exponent of 2 in phi(n) is at least 1.
For all n > 2 with exactly one odd prime divisor, vp(phi(n), 2) >= 1.
predicate: (n <= 2) or (len([p for p in range(3, int(math.sqrt(n))+1, 2) if is_prime(p) and n % p == 0]) != 1) or (vp(phi(n), 2) >= 1)

claim: For all n > 2 that are not powers of 2, phi(n) is divisible by at least 2.
For all n > 2, phi(n) divides the sum of all divisors of n when n is prime.
For all n > 2, phi(n) * tau(n) > n where tau is the number of divisors.
For all n > 3, phi(n) >= sqrt(n/2).
For all n > 2, tau(n) < 2*n/phi(n).
For all n > 2, sum of phi(d) for d dividing n equals n.
predicate: (n <= 2) or (sum(phi(d) for d in range(1, n+1) if n % d == 0) == n)

claim: For all n > 2 with an odd divisor d > 1, phi(d) is even.
predicate: (n <= 2) or (all(phi(d) % 2 == 0 for d in range(3, n+1, 2) if n % d == 0 and d > 1)) or (not any(d > 1 for d in range(3, n+1, 2) if n % d == 0))

claim: For all n > 2, phi(n) divides sigma(n) when n has a special divisibility property.
For all n > 2, the ratio phi(n)/n has denominator even.
For all odd n > 2, phi(4n) = 2*phi(n).
For all odd n > 2, phi(2^k * n) = 2^(k-1) * phi(n) for k >= 1.
predicate: (n <= 2) or (n % 2 == 0) or (all(phi(2**k * n) == 2**(max(0, k-1)) * phi(n) for k in range(1, 5)))

claim: For all n > 2, phi(n) is even implies n is not a prime power of an odd prime > 2.
predicate: (n <= 2) or (phi(n) % 2 == 1) or (not any(is_prime(p) and n == p**int(math.log(n, p)+0.5) for p in range(3, int(math.sqrt(n))+1, 2)))

claim: For all n > 2 with phi(n) even, n has either factor 2 or at least two distinct odd prime divisors.
predicate: (n <= 2) or (phi(n) % 2 == 1) or (n % 2 == 0) or (len([p for p in range(3, int(math.sqrt(n))+1, 2) if is_prime(p) and n % p == 0]) >= 2)

claim: For all n > 2 not a power of 2, phi(n) is even due to presence of odd prime factors.
For all n > 2, phi(n^2) = n * phi(n).
For all n > 2, phi(n^3) = n^2 * phi(n).
For all n > 2 and k >= 2, phi(n^k) = n^(k-1) * phi(n).
predicate: (n <= 2) or (all(phi(n**k) == n**(k-1) * phi(n) for k in range(2, 6)))

claim: For all n > 2, the equation x^(phi(n)) ≡ 1 (mod n) has exactly phi(n) solutions.
For all n > 2, 2*phi(n) < 2*n with equality only for n = 3.
For all n > 2 with odd prime p | n, vp(phi(n), 2) >= 1 + vp(n, p) when p-1 is even.
predicate: (n <= 2) or (all(vp(phi(n), 2) >= 1 for p in range(3, int(math.sqrt(n))+1, 2) if is_prime(p) and n % p == 0) or not any(is_prime(p) and n % p == 0 for p in range(3, int(math.sqrt(n))+1, 2)))

claim: For all n > 2, phi(n) + phi(n) is divisible by 2.
For all n > 2, the parity structure of phi(n) forces n to have either 2 as a factor or multiple odd prime divisors.
predicate: (n <= 2) or (n % 2 == 0) or (len([p for p in range(3, int(math.sqrt(n))+1, 2) if is_prime(p) and n % p == 0]) >= 1)

claim: For all n > 2, if n = 2^a * m where m is odd, then phi(n) = 2^(a-1) * phi(m) for a >= 1.
